title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the i-th day, return the maximum profit you can achieve from this transaction. If you cannot achieve any profit, return 0. You must buy before you sell.

examples.explanation: Buy on day 2 (price = 1) and sell on day 5 (price = 6). Profit = 6 - 1 = 5.
In this case, no transactions are done and the max profit is 0 because the prices only decrease.
Buy on day 1 (price = 1) and sell on day 5 (price = 5). Profit = 5 - 1 = 4.

constraints: The length of prices will be in the range [0, 10^5].
prices[i] is a positive integer and will not exceed 10^4.
hints: Consider maintaining a variable for the minimum price seen so far.
Calculate the potential profit at each price point.
Update the maximum profit whenever you find a better opportunity.
Think about edge cases like decreasing prices or constant prices.
